questions.text: Silkworm larvae used in sericulture primarily feed on the leaves of which plant?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Mulberry plant.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Silkworm (Bombyx mori) larvae feed almost exclusively on mulberry leaves. Mulberry leaves contain the essential nutrients and proteins required for silk production. The silk protein produced by silkworm consists mainly of fibroin and sericin. This is the scientific basis of sericulture, where mulberry plantations are cultivated specifically to raise silkworms.

Explanation of Other Options:
Reetha plant: Soapnut plant leaves are not suitable for silkworm feeding and are not used in sericulture.
Peach plant: Although some silkworm species may survive temporarily on other leaves, commercial sericulture relies almost entirely on mulberry.
Ficus plant: Ficus leaves are not nutritionally appropriate for silkworm growth.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Scientific name of silkworm: Bombyx mori
• Silk is obtained during the pupal stage of the life cycle.
• Rearing of silkworms is called Sericulture.
• Mulberry belongs to the family Moraceae.

questions.text: Quinine, an important antimalarial drug, is obtained from which group of organisms?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Angiosperms.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Quinine is an alkaloid extracted from the bark of the Cinchona tree. Cinchona is a flowering plant belonging to angiosperms. The drug was historically the most effective treatment for malaria before synthetic drugs were developed.

Explanation of Other Options:
Bacteria: Produce antibiotics like penicillin but not quinine.
Yeast: Used in fermentation processes but not in quinine production.
Algae: Produce bioactive compounds but quinine is not among them.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Botanical name of Cinchona: Cinchona officinalis.
• Quinine is an alkaloid.
• Alkaloids are nitrogen-containing plant secondary metabolites.
• Other plant alkaloids: Morphine (opium poppy), Nicotine (tobacco), Caffeine (coffee).

questions.text: Quinine used in malaria treatment is extracted from which part of the Cinchona plant?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Bark.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Quinine accumulates in the bark of the Cinchona tree. The bark is dried and processed to extract quinine alkaloids which are used to treat malaria.

Explanation of Other Options:
Leaves: Contain negligible quantities of quinine.
Roots: Not used for quinine extraction.
Flowers: Have no medicinal role related to quinine.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Other drugs from plant bark include: Cinnamon (spice), Cinchona (quinine).
• Alkaloids are often stored in plant bark as defense chemicals.

questions.text: Arteether, a modern antimalarial drug, is derived from which source?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Seed plant.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Arteether is derived from Artemisinin, which is obtained from the plant Artemisia annua (sweet wormwood). Artemisinin derivatives are currently used in ACT therapy for malaria.

Explanation of Other Options:
Fungus: Some antibiotics are produced by fungi, but not arteether.
Bacteria: Known for antibiotic production but not artemisinin.
Algae: Produce pigments and bioactive compounds but not arteether.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Artemisinin discovery led to a Nobel Prize in Medicine (Tu Youyou).
• Artemisia annua belongs to the family Asteraceae.

questions.text: Which synthetic drug replaced natural quinine for malaria treatment?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Chloroquine.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Chloroquine is a synthetic antimalarial drug developed to replace quinine because natural quinine extraction was expensive and limited.

Explanation of Other Options:
Tetracycline: Antibiotic used against bacterial infections.
Ampicillin: Penicillin-group antibiotic.
Chloromycetin: Another name for chloramphenicol.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Resistance to chloroquine has developed in many malaria parasites.
• Artemisinin-based therapies are now widely used.

questions.text: Which microscopic organisms are used in forensic science to diagnose death by drowning?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Diatoms.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Diatoms are microscopic algae with silica cell walls. If a person drowns, water containing diatoms enters the lungs and bloodstream and may reach bone marrow. Their presence helps confirm drowning.

Explanation of Other Options:
Lichens: Symbiotic organisms found on rocks and trees.
Protozoa: Single-celled organisms not used in drowning diagnosis.
Cyanobacteria: Photosynthetic bacteria but not forensic markers.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Diatoms are important components of phytoplankton.
• They play a key role in aquatic food chains.

questions.text: Mulberry fruit is botanically classified as which type of fruit?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Sorosis.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Sorosis is a multiple fruit formed by the fusion of ovaries from many flowers of an inflorescence. Examples include mulberry, pineapple, and jackfruit.

Explanation of Other Options:
Syconus: Fruit type of fig.
Samara: Winged fruit like maple.
Nut: Hard single-seeded fruit.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Types of fruits: Simple, aggregate, and multiple fruits.
• Example of aggregate fruit: Strawberry.

questions.text: Which of the following fibres is NOT obtained from the stem of a plant?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Cotton.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Cotton fibres come from the seed hairs of the cotton plant.

Explanation of Other Options:
Flax: Stem fibre.
Hemp: Bast fibre obtained from the stem.
Jute: Stem fibre widely used in ropes and sacks.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Cotton mainly consists of cellulose.
• Cotton plant belongs to genus Gossypium.

questions.text: Leghemoglobin is found in which plant structure?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Root nodules.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Leghemoglobin is a pigment present in root nodules of leguminous plants where Rhizobium bacteria fix nitrogen. It regulates oxygen levels to protect the nitrogenase enzyme.

Explanation of Other Options:
Leaves, stems, and flowers do not contain leghemoglobin.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Nitrogen fixation converts atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia.
• Examples of legumes: Pea, gram, soybean.

questions.text: Which cereal crop is artificially produced through hybridization of wheat and rye?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Triticale.

Explanation of Correct Option:
Triticale is a hybrid cereal produced by crossing wheat (Triticum) and rye (Secale). It combines the productivity of wheat and the hardiness of rye.

Explanation of Other Options:
Maize: Natural cereal crop.
Barley: Natural cereal species.
Soybean: Leguminous crop.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Hybridization is used in crop improvement.
• Other hybrid crops include hybrid maize.

questions.text: Canola oil varieties are specially bred to contain:
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Very low erucic acid.

Explanation:
Canola is a genetically improved variety of rapeseed oil developed to reduce erucic acid content, making the oil safer for human consumption.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Canola stands for Canadian Oil Low Acid.

questions.text: Bacteria can normally be seen using which instrument?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Compound microscope.

Explanation:
Bacteria are microscopic organisms typically measuring 1–5 micrometers. They are visible under a compound light microscope.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Viruses require electron microscopes for observation.

questions.text: Spongy tissue disorder affecting mango exports mainly occurs in which variety?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Alphonso.

Explanation:
Spongy tissue is a physiological disorder of Alphonso mango that affects fruit quality and export potential.

questions.text: Which statement about bacteria is correct?
options: They lie between living and non-living organisms
They lie between plants and animals
They lie between flowering and non-flowering plants
questions.solution:
Let’s dive into this specific problem about **mango varieties and agricultural science**, which is a great example of the **General Science** and **General Awareness** topics often found in the **DRDO CEPTAM-11 STA-B exam**. I’ll include guiding ideas along the way to help connect these facts with broader exam concepts.

### 🥭 Understanding the Problem: Spongy Tissue Disorder

The question asks which mango variety is most affected by **spongy tissue disorder**, a **non-infectious metabolic disorder** that causes the pulp to become **soft, acidic, and unmarketable**.

• **(A) Alphonso:** This variety, mainly grown in the **Konkan region of Maharashtra**, is famous worldwide for export quality but is also **most susceptible to spongy tissue disorder**. The disorder is often linked to **post-harvest heat radiation from the soil**.

• **(B) Langra:** A popular **North Indian mango variety (Varanasi region)** known for its **green skin and strong aroma**. It is generally **resistant to this specific disorder**.

• **(C) Dashehari:** Another well-known **North Indian mango variety from Malihabad (Uttar Pradesh)**. Like Langra, it **does not show high susceptibility** to spongy tissue disorder.

• **(D) Neelum:** A **late-season mango variety** with good shelf life. It is sometimes used in **breeding programs to develop hybrid varieties resistant to disorders**.

------------------------------

### 🏛️ DRDO CEPTAM Context: Concepts & Potential Questions

In the **CEPTAM STA-B exam**, this type of question falls under **Applied Biology and Agricultural Science**. DRDO examinations often test knowledge of **India’s agricultural resources and scientific issues affecting productivity and exports**.

| Concept | Potential Exam Question |
|---|---|
| Plant Pathology | "Which physiological disorder in mango is caused by high temperature and convective heat?" |
| Economic Botany | "Which Indian mango variety has received a GI (Geographical Indication) tag and is a major export product?" |
| General Science | "Metabolic disorders in plants are often caused by deficiencies of which micronutrients?" |

------------------------------

### 🧠 Conceptual Thinking Step

To understand why **Alphonso** is the correct answer, focus on the **cause of the disorder** mentioned earlier: **soil heat radiation**.

Competitive exams like **DRDO CEPTAM** frequently test **cause-and-effect reasoning in science**.

If agricultural scientists discovered that **covering the soil with dry grass (mulching)** reduces the occurrence of this disorder in **Alphonso mangoes**, then the agricultural practice is likely aimed at regulating a **physical property of the soil**, such as:

• **Soil temperature regulation**
• **Reduction of heat radiation from soil**
• **Moisture retention and insulation of the soil surface**

Understanding such relationships between **environmental factors and plant physiology** is important for solving **applied science questions in competitive exams**.

questions.text: Desert plants often develop very long roots mainly because:
options: Roots grow deep in search of water
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Roots grow deep in search of water.

Explanation:
Desert plants extend their roots deep into the soil to access underground water sources.

questions.text: Phreatophytes are plants characterized by:
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Very deep tap roots.

Explanation:
Phreatophytes develop extremely long roots that reach groundwater.

questions.text: Plants adapted to dry climates are called:
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Xerophytes.

Explanation:
Xerophytes are plants adapted to dry conditions and show features such as thick cuticles and water storage tissues.

questions.text: Plants that grow in saline soil are known as:
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Halophytes.

Explanation:
Halophytes tolerate high salt concentrations. Examples include mangroves.

questions.text: Most desert plants store water in their tissues. Such plants are called:
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Succulent plants.

Explanation:
Succulent plants store water in stems or leaves to survive long dry periods.

questions.text: The pungency of chilli peppers is caused by which chemical compound?
questions.solution:
Correct Answer: Capsaicin.

Explanation:
Capsaicin is an alkaloid compound responsible for the hot taste of chillies.

Possible Exam Extensions:
• Lycopene gives red colour to tomatoes.
• Carotene gives orange colour to carrots.
• Anthocyanin produces red/blue colours in plants.
